decision.title: Use XML for planning docs going forward
decision.summary: Planning artifacts (plan/summary/roadmap/tasks/decisions/state) must be maintained in XML templates with composable structure.
decision.impact: Improves searchability, allows agents to compose/parse sections, and keeps loop artifacts compact.

decision.title: Loop must ask "what can improve?"
decision.summary: Each phase includes an improvement self-check covering UX, performance, maintenance, and compactness.
decision.impact: Prevents phase drift and encourages refactoring toward smaller, clearer systems.

decision.title: PRD/GRD references are mandatory in each loop
decision.summary: Each phase plan/summary must cite PRD/GRD (or other direction docs) to keep work aligned with overall direction.
decision.impact: Prevents local optimizations from drifting from design intent.

decision.title: Agents must register unique ids before claiming tasks
decision.summary: Each agent generates a unique id and records it in STATE.xml to avoid collisions across concurrent phases.
decision.impact: Improves concurrency safety and makes task ownership discoverable.

decision.title: Planning management via CLI + MCP server
decision.summary: Planning updates and snapshots are managed through a dedicated CLI and MCP server to support multi-agent coordination.
decision.impact: Reduces manual edits and enables automated agent coordination tooling.

decision.title: Ralph Wiggum loop for greenfield overnight automation
decision.summary: Iterative agent loop until a completion promise (e.g. <promise>COMPLETE</promise>) appears in output. Progress persisted via git each iteration; context resets are acceptable. Use for greenfield work: fresh repos, TDD, verifiable tasks. Not for subjective design or production deploys.
decision.impact: Enables unattended overnight runs; pair with git worktrees for parallel greenfield builds. Task spec and promise live in a task file (e.g. RALPH_TASK.md); script runs agent, checks output, commits and repeats until promise or max iterations.

decision.title: Consolidate planning markdown into REQUIREMENTS.xml
decision.summary: Root planning markdown is migrated into `REQUIREMENTS.xml` so XML is the single source.
decision.impact: All root planning data is unified in XML, eliminating duplicate sources.

decision.title: Space Explorer panels require stable IDs and named headers
decision.summary: Interactive Space Explorer panels use explicit `id` / `data-ui-id` attributes and titled headers so future change requests can target exact UI regions.
decision.impact: Improves collaboration speed, reduces ambiguous feedback loops, and keeps visual guidance consistent through context-colored info help controls.

decision.title: Deltas is a primary visualization mode
decision.summary: Space Explorer uses one visualization viewport where `3D`, `JSON`, and `Deltas` all switch the same panel surface; reachability information is represented as compact, icon-coded header badges.
decision.impact: Removes split-panel confusion, increases compactness, and keeps context visible while editing deltas.

decision.title: Space View is the only space selector
decision.summary: `Combined` was promoted into Space View, the separate Content Space selector was removed, and content subsets (`Skill`, `Dialogue`, `Archetype`) became first-class modes using the trait-feature vector path.
decision.impact: Eliminates redundant selection, improves clarity, and keeps nearest/visualization data aligned with the chosen space.

decision.title: Space vectors are feature-assigned per space
decision.summary: Space Explorer now treats each content space as an assigned feature vector (including Fame/Awareness/Guile), with a dedicated Define Feature modal and movement vector feature assignment controls.
decision.impact: Removes static panel ambiguity, supports shared-feature cross-space visualization, and provides an explicit authoring loop for new space features.

decision.title: Active Kael model is always visible from controls
decision.summary: Model Schema Builder now includes a dedicated popup and explicitly displays the active Kael model (`entity.*` inference with base fallback) so schema inspection has clear context.
decision.impact: Reduces ambiguity about which model is being inspected and accelerates schema iteration feedback loops.

decision.title: Model schema selection uses hierarchical tree navigation
decision.summary: The Model Schema popup uses `react-arborist` and groups model IDs by namespace prefix to expose hierarchy (`entity.*`, `item.*`, `room.*`, etc.).
decision.impact: Improves comprehension of schema taxonomy and reduces cognitive load versus a flat list.

decision.title: Named model instances live under model nodes and can be canonical content
decision.summary: Model tree includes instance children (e.g., Kael) under model schemas; instances support canonical toggle and are serialized in patch exports under `contentBindings`.
decision.impact: Clarifies model-vs-instance hierarchy and allows canonical instance bindings to persist as content artifacts.

decision.title: Model viewer interactions use dedicated zustand slice
decision.summary: Model viewer selection/language/tree instance state moved to a dedicated zustand+immer store with shallow selection to avoid full Space Explorer rerenders on tree clicks.
decision.impact: Improves click responsiveness in schema tree and isolates heavy visualization computation from modal interactions.

decision.title: Authoring state persists locally with migration script visibility
decision.summary: Space Explorer UI/model viewer stores persist to browser storage; drag-moving canonical instances between models records migration operations and exposes copyable migration script/code blocks.
decision.impact: Prevents work loss across reloads and makes schema reassignment traceable for code/content migrations.

decision.title: Model schema handoff is file-based with inherited defaults
decision.summary: Model Schema Viewer emits representative TypeScript/C++/C# files across the active inheritance chain and allows per-feature ref default overrides in the same panel.
decision.impact: Improves developer handoff quality by showing concrete file structure, explicit inheritance links, and default values used by canonical assets.

decision.title: Remove ambiguous marker-grouping controls from Space Explorer
decision.summary: 3D marker grouping selector is removed from controls and marker coloring is fixed to branch so the plot behavior is predictable; Space Features removes active-space banner and feature-assign controls.
decision.impact: Reduces UI noise and confusion while keeping direct vector editing focused on current model-space view.

decision.title: Model Space View options derive from active model schema
decision.summary: Space view selection changed from fixed card grid to a dropdown whose options are computed from the active model schema feature-ref spaces, with `(content-combined)` always available.
decision.impact: Keeps exploration controls aligned with model schema definitions and reduces disconnect between schema authoring and runtime space selection.

decision.title: Model selection and creation are centered in schema workflow
decision.summary: Space controls include an explicit model picker from runtime model schemas (same source as model tree), and model tree modal now allows creating new model schemas using current schema as template.
decision.impact: Improves schema authoring flow, keeps selection and creation in one model-centric loop, and avoids hidden/legacy model creation paths.

decision.title: Model tree is the source of model-space selection and multi-model overlays
decision.summary: Controls now defer model selection to the Model Tree workflow, and model nodes include checkboxes for selecting multiple models to overlay in the 3D viewer for the active space.
decision.impact: Unifies model selection UX and enables direct comparison of multiple model spaces without switching context.

decision.title: Feature controls are grouped by inheritance levels
decision.summary: Model Space section controls were removed and feature controls are now grouped by inheritance chain levels (`base`, class layers), while model-space overlays are auto-generated from the same inheritance chain.
decision.impact: Makes base/class composition explicit and avoids duplicate model-space selection controls.

decision.title: Add reusable combatstats inheritance root for entity/item schemas
decision.summary: Runtime schema normalization now injects default stat features and a `combatstats` model, with `entity.base` and `item.base` explicitly extending it through `extendsModelId`, and schema code generation honors explicit inheritance links.
decision.impact: Improves schema reuse and developer handoff by centralizing default combat stats and making inheritance explicit across namespaces.

decision.title: Stat Control is flattened and includes CurrencyStats inheritance
decision.summary: Space Movement controls and merge-inbox panel are removed from the control stack; stat sliders are flattened into Stat Control. Added `currencystats` reusable stat schema and `currency` model extending it so model vectors are expressed as normal stats.
decision.impact: Reduces UI noise and aligns model vectors with a reusable stat-schema inheritance pattern.

decision.title: Pack/report hash binding is explicit and model selection is canonical-first
decision.summary: Space Explorer captures loaded content-pack identity and loaded report binding, displays hash mismatch state, and defaults stat-model selectors to canonical model instances. CI and release workflows emit `play-report-binding` artifacts linked to content-pack report hashes.
decision.impact: Improves traceability between authored content packs and agent playthrough analysis while reducing accidental selection of non-canonical models.

decision.title: Content packs now publish manifest + schema bundle + language stubs
decision.summary: `content-pack.manifest.v1` is generated from bundle space vectors (models/stat classes/canonical assets/spaces), release artifacts include JSON schema bundle and TS/C++/C# model stubs, and play-report API backfills pack binding from current loaded bundle when report metadata is missing.
decision.impact: Aligns Space Explorer authoring with engine/runtime consumption and makes cross-engine handoff deterministic, portable, and traceable by pack hash/version.

decision.title: PRD and requirements: one reusable doc vs many; when to split
decision.summary: Prefer a single REQUIREMENTS.xml (or one requirements “bundle” per product) until scope clearly splits. One doc is fine for: one product, one platform supporting that product, or a small set of related products. Split when: (1) a new product or platform has a distinct stakeholder and release cadence, (2) token/size limits make a single doc unwieldy for context (use planning context tokens to monitor), or (3) teams work in non-overlapping requirement sets. Platform requirements (infra, tooling, MCP, CI) can live in the same doc as product requirements as long as they are clearly sections; when the platform becomes a first-class “product” with its own roadmap, consider a separate PLATFORM-REQUIREMENTS or split REQUIREMENTS by domain. No fixed token cap—use “can we still load and reason about this in one context?” as the signal; when in doubt, split by product/platform boundary rather than by size alone.
decision.impact: Keeps context manageable; avoids premature fragmentation; gives a clear rule for when to create a new requirements doc or restructure (e.g. project → product → platform with multiple products). Planning CLI token report (context tokens) supports monitoring PRD/REQUIREMENTS size.

decision.title: Phase 50 uses Assistant UI + Codex app server with account-linked subscription boundaries
decision.summary: AI integration planning is anchored on Assistant UI for conversation UX and Codex CLI/app server for execution/orchestration paths, while preserving user account/subscription boundaries instead of embedding shared API keys directly in client code.
decision.impact: Implementation tasks must treat auth/session and entitlement checks as first-class architecture concerns, and schema editing flows must run through server-validated endpoints.

decision.title: Use REQUIREMENTS.xml as temporary direction source when REQUIREMENTS.xml is absent
decision.summary: The loop currently points to `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml`, but that file is not present; Phase 50 planning uses `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml` plus existing roadmap/decisions as authoritative references until a dedicated REQUIREMENTS.xml is restored.
decision.impact: Prevents planning stalls and keeps PRD/GRD citation requirements enforceable with currently available XML sources.

decision.title: Product Owner agent as human stand-in for planning; all agents talk to it
decision.summary: A dedicated “Product Owner” agent is the human stand-in for managing agents and planning: it answers questions, directs work, and updates decisions/requirements. All agents (including Codex CLI / app server agents) can talk to it via MCP or the Codex SDK agent channel. It is implemented and exposed as part of Phase 50 (Assistant UI + Codex app server), so the same integration stack (MCP server, Codex agents) is reused. The Product Owner does not replace human input for major scope changes—it reduces the need for humans to manually read/write planning docs for routine coordination.
decision.impact: Phase 50 deliverables must include an agent role or MCP tooling that can act as this Product Owner; planning-mcp and/or Codex app server agents should be aligned so one agent can fulfill this role. Enables “ask the Product Owner” workflow for any agent.

decision.title: Questions per phase drive decisions and requirements updates
decision.summary: Each phase maintains a set of open questions (in the phase PLAN or a linked questions doc). As questions are answered (by humans or the Product Owner agent), answers are reflected in DECISIONS.xml and REQUIREMENTS.xml so the planning loop stays current. This aligns with Phase 50 and the Product Owner: the PO can own updating questions and propagating answers into decisions/requirements.
decision.impact: Phase plans should include a <questions> section (or equivalent); templates and CLI can support listing/updating phase questions. Keeps planning docs from drifting and gives a single place to “what do we still need to decide?” per phase.

decision.title: Phase 50 executes behind server-controlled feature flags
decision.summary: All new AI integration surfaces (Assistant UI session routing, subscription gating, schema mutation APIs, and report-assisted flows) must ship behind server-controlled flags with explicit cohorts (internal, pilot, general) and kill-switch behavior.
decision.impact: Allows rapid shipping while containing blast radius and supports reversible rollout without reverting code.

decision.title: Phase 50 uses three acceleration lanes with exit criteria
decision.summary: Implementation planning uses lane A (first-party fast path), lane B (hybrid with third-party managed components), and lane C (managed third-party heavy) with explicit switch criteria based on delivery speed, operational risk, and lock-in tolerance.
decision.impact: Keeps delivery velocity high while preserving an intentional build-vs-buy decision and documented fallback path.

decision.title: AI feature flags live in docs-site TOML config files with server-side evaluation
decision.summary: Canonical flag defaults are stored in `docs-site/config/ai-flags.defaults.toml`; optional developer overrides live in `docs-site/config/ai-flags.local.toml` (gitignored) and an optional env-specified TOML path can override both. All evaluation is server-side.
decision.impact: Provides explicit, reviewable defaults while enabling safe local experimentation and emergency overrides without client-side trust.

decision.title: Loop templates include requriements-suggestions block for requirement gap capture
decision.summary: PLAN, SUMMARY, and LOOP templates now include a `requriements-suggestions` section so agents capture requirement gaps and concrete update proposals while continuing execution.
decision.impact: Makes requirement maintenance a built-in loop output and reduces stalls from unresolved documentation gaps.

decision.title: Default acceleration lane is B (hybrid third-party)
decision.summary: Phase 50 closes with lane B as default execution path: keep orchestration and policy boundaries in app server while allowing selected third-party components for delivery speed.
decision.impact: Balances near-term velocity with controllable lock-in and a clear path back to first-party operation.

decision.title: Phase 50 is not complete until Space Explorer chat authoring ships end-to-end
decision.summary: Planning artifacts alone do not satisfy Phase 50. Completion now requires: in-app chat interface in Space Explorer, Codex-backed conversation capable of discussing/editing models/stats/content schema, canonical asset creation, and serialization into content bundle outputs (not schema-only).
decision.impact: Reopens Phase 50 execution tasks and resets DoD to product behavior verification rather than planning closure.

decision.title: Space Explorer chat authoring uses a reusable panel component
decision.summary: Rather than embedding chat logic directly in Space Explorer, Phase 50 implements a reusable `AuthoringChatPanel` component and mounts it in Space Explorer with context payloads. Server route `/api/ai/space-authoring-chat` handles model calls and feature-flag gating.
decision.impact: Keeps heavy Space Explorer iteration manageable and enables reuse of chat authoring in additional surfaces without duplicating state/network logic.

decision.title: Space Explorer chat trigger is a floating Assistant UI modal widget using shadcn controls
decision.summary: Phase 50 chat UX now uses a bottom-right floating widget (`AuthoringAssistantWidget`) with `AssistantModalPrimitive` for open/close and modal positioning, while retaining the reusable `AuthoringChatPanel` as modal content. Inline/embedded chat controls were removed from Space Explorer.
decision.impact: Aligns chat UX with Assistant UI modal patterns, keeps Space Explorer less crowded, and enforces shadcn-first button/input usage for this phase's new UI surface.

decision.title: Space Explorer chat uses structured operations with explicit apply step
decision.summary: Phase 50 chat responses now carry typed operation payloads (models/features/canonical assets/bundle build). The UI shows proposed operations and applies them only on explicit user action, then returns validation/build feedback.
decision.impact: Moves chat from advisory text to safe, auditable authoring mutations while preserving user control and enabling bundle-serialization actions from the same conversation flow.

decision.title: Phase 52 uses encrypted env bundle artifacts for cross-repo secret transport
decision.summary: Docs-site and Unreal plugin secret exchange is standardized on `scripts/env-bundle.mjs` using AES-256-GCM sealed bundles (`.secrets/env.bundle.sealed.json`) with key material provided only from local shell/CI secret stores.
decision.impact: Reduces plaintext-env handling across repos and creates a repeatable, auditable workflow while Supabase distribution and plugin ingestion contracts are being implemented.

decision.title: Use Supabase/S3 for distribution and Dolt for optional patch lineage
decision.summary: Phase 52 delivery architecture uses Supabase Storage (S3-compatible) as the runtime distribution channel for signed downloadable pack artifacts, while Dolt remains an optional local/build-time source for content patch history merged by ingest workflows.
decision.impact: Avoids introducing Dolt as a runtime dependency for Unreal plugin import/download while preserving existing Dolt-backed authoring/version workflows.

decision.title: Split Dolt deep workflow design into dedicated Phase 53
decision.summary: Phase 52 continues with runtime delivery (Supabase publish/pull contracts, Unreal consumption, security, observability), while Dolt governance and promotion policy are explicitly tracked in a new planned Phase 53.
decision.impact: Maintains current delivery momentum and prevents Dolt governance scope from blocking downloadable pack delivery.

decision.title: RepoPlanner package owns all planning API surface; host installs via install-routes
decision.summary: Planning-related API handlers (test-reports/unit, planning-state, planning-cli, etc.) live in vendor/repo-planner/api/. The host Next.js app does not own this logic: it runs install-routes.mjs to write thin re-export route files that delegate to the package. Package can run standalone with REPOPLANNER_PROJECT_ROOT for project-folder selection.
decision.impact: Single source of truth for planning API; host gets updates by re-running install-routes; package is embeddable or standalone.

decision.title: Greenfield vs brownfield for dependency analysis and multi-project cockpit
decision.summary: Greenfield = fresh/new codebases (e.g. reverse-engineering from node_modules to extract feature requirements). Brownfield = existing codebases (normal workflow: analyze usage, assess replaceability). Cockpit can center on any folder that contains .planning (project root); treat each such root as a switchable project so we can work on replacement repos or main repo interchangeably. Projects discovered during existing repo scans (no saved list).
decision.impact: Phase 55 and later: dependency analysis pipeline, requirements in replacement repo REQUIREMENTS.xml, lightweight-rebuild roadmap; project discovery from scans.

decision.title: All planning and tasks use loop XML and planning CLI; no ad-hoc planning docs
decision.summary: Planning, tasks, requirements, roadmap, and decisions use .planning XML documents and the planning CLI workflow. Do not create one-off markdown or standalone planning documents; use REQUIREMENTS.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml, and phase PLAN/SUMMARY from templates. Replacement repos (e.g. for dependency rebuilds) get the same .planning structure and CLI.
decision.impact: Prevents document drift and keeps a single source of truth; agents and humans follow the same loop; dependency requirements live in replacement repo's REQUIREMENTS.xml (our structure).

decision.title: Cockpit: 5 main tabs with sub-views; semantic color theming and icon usage
decision.summary: Planning cockpit uses 5 main tabs (Overview, Work, State, Tools, Chat) with sub-views where applicable (e.g. Overview = Dashboard | Reports). Second row shows sub-triggers (pills) with icons. All status badges use statusVariant/statusClassName for semantic coloring (done=green, in-progress=amber, failed=red). Icons: one per tab/sub (lucide size-3.5 main, size-3 sub); labels always present. Continue to revisit and refactor layouts for compactness and consistency.
decision.impact: Reduces tab clutter; clear hierarchy; consistent theming and icon usage across cockpit; layouts doc and STYLING.md guide future refactors.

decision.title: Cockpit: phase↔task drill-down, tooltips, motion, icon/color conventions
decision.summary: Phase drill-down: Tasks filterable by phase (click phase in table or in Phases/Questions); Phases tab has “By phase” list with “View tasks (n)”; filter chip with clear when active. Tooltips: native title on metric cards (PlanningMetricCard tooltip prop), section HelpCircle icons with title. Motion: tab content wrapped in motion.div (motion/react) with key=contentKey, fade-in ~150ms. Icons/colors: documented in PLANNING-COCKPIT-REQUIREMENTS 2.10 (LayoutGrid=phases, ListTodo=tasks, HelpCircle=questions, etc.; status via planning-status).
decision.impact: Easier navigation from phase to tasks and back; less wordy UI; consistent transitions and entity visuals.
